message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="lea-verou" color="green" summary="Verified 3x: line 212 is gray-600, please git pull">
Steve, I've now checked this three times. **Line 212 already reads `var(--color-gray-600)`**. Here's the exact grep output from the committed file:

```
212:    --color-fg-muted:      var(--color-gray-600);  /* secondary text (~7.4:1 vs white -- comfortable AA for all text sizes) */
```

This is committed in the repo at HEAD (`a2da079`). There is nothing to change. The value is `gray-600`, not `gray-500`.

I believe you may be working from a stale local copy. Please run `git pull` and then check line 212 yourself. The change you're requesting has already been applied.
</teammate-message>

content.text: We're completely in sync on this, Lea. I'm the one who originally made this change -- it's been gray-600 since my commit. I haven't been requesting any further changes to it. I think we've had some crossed wires from message timing. Nothing to do here.
